checks.a_analysis: The HTML document significantly fails to represent the visual organization of the source. The body content begins abruptly with content from the middle of the document (Page 7/Page 11), skipping the first 6 pages which include the masthead, leadership messages, and community letters. A large portion of the regional reports (Eastern and North Central) is also missing. This is further highlighted by a note at the end of the HTML stating that pages 1-11 were 'unavailable' for conversion, representing a major failure in visual flow and information completeness compared to the source's intent.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF begins with the 'NC 911 Board Monthly Dispatch' masthead (Page 1), messages from the Governor and Board Chair (Page 2), and a formal letter to the community (Page 4).
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML body starts with 'In the Field... (cont.)' and 'The Western Region Report'. At the bottom of the page, it states: '[Pages 1-11 unavailable] Content for pages 1-11 could not be converted.'

checks.a_analysis: The source document does not contain traditional grid-based tables with borders. Data presented as lists (such as training dates on page 21) are rendered clearly in the HTML as structured text lists. There are no corrupted table structures.

While the HTML correctly identified where images should be and provided captions, the vast majority of the meaningful images (photographs of personnel, meeting events, and PSAP visits) are missing. They are rendered as empty green placeholder boxes. This includes images from pages 11-26. This loss of visual data significantly degrades the 'Highlights' and 'In the Field' sections of the newsletter.
checks.b_evidence_source: The source PDF contains numerous meaningful photos such as staff at PSAPs (Pages 9, 10, 12, 13), students participating in programs (Pages 7, 11), and renovation progress (Page 17).
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML renders these as green rectangular boxes, such as those labeled 'Cheesing with Sarge in Yancey County!', 'Yancey County PSAP', 'Buncombe County 911 PSAP Assessment', and 'Renovations Continue....'
checks.b_evidence_location: Throughout document body starting from 'The Western Region Report'

checks.a_analysis: The body content in the HTML output follows a linear progression (albeit with missing sections) without repeating paragraphs, images, or tables. Navigation elements (TOC) correctly link to body headings without duplicating the content itself.
